workLog.task: Issue #2001: spec-generator agent emits invalid frontmatter enums (priority=medium, category=tooling, status=ready) because it writes frontmatter from memory instead of reading the schema (PR #1995 drew 9 schema-violation threads; PR #1989 same)
workLog.outcome: Converted spec-generator from an agent to a skill at .claude/skills/spec-generator/. Bundled .agents/governance/spec-schemas.md as references/spec-schemas.md and added a deterministic validate_spec_frontmatter.py that mirrors the schema enums verbatim (canonical-source-mirror compliant). The SKILL.md makes reading the schema and running the validator a BLOCKING gate. Added a 20-case contract test pinning the documented drift as negative cases. Rerouted /spec, orchestrator, retrospective, and the sibling skills from Task(spec-generator) to Skill(spec-generator); removed the agent; updated docs and the 4 plugin/marketplace manifests; ran the ADR-057 eval on the behavioral edits.
workLog.evidence: pytest .claude/skills/spec-generator/tests -> 20 passed; validator rejects priority=medium/category=tooling/status=ready; install-parity OK

nextSteps: Post-merge: confirm /spec end-to-end produces schema-valid specs in a fresh run
